pattern: Tracking the longest run ending on an up-step versus a down-step lets a single greedy pass count alternating differences in linear time.
hints: Only the direction of each consecutive difference matters, so think about how the best subsequence grows when the next move goes up versus when it goes down.
Keep two running lengths: the longest wiggle ending with an upward step and the longest ending with a downward step, and update each from the other when the sign flips.
Walk left to right; when the current value rises set up = down + 1, when it falls set down = up + 1, ignore equal neighbours, and answer with the larger of up and down.

edge_inputs.description: strictly increasing collapses to length two
all equal values give length one
duplicates between turns must not count as a wiggle

stress.description: random small-range values so many plateaus and turns occur
stress.note: tiny value range maximises equal-neighbour and turn stress
